MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-13-296725 Conservatorship of Thea Archilletti and PTR-14-298368 The Thea T. Archilletti Revocable Trust.

Appearances: Ernest Der appeared by zoom for and with Paula Bibbs (Conservator/Trustee) by zoom.

The Court noted that the sale order was filed on February 24th, that escrow was to close within 30-days and asked if it closed as scheduled. Mr. Der stated that the sale closed as scheduled, that the petition for the conservatorship is drafted and that he can have it filed by the end of next week. The Court questions whether there are any difficulties that are causing the delay. Ms. Bibbs stated that she is having a difficult time with balancing the accounting, that she is waiting for additional information and that she is waiting for one more statement from Principal Finance. Ms. Bibbs also stated that Accountant Martin Harris has agreed to take this on to assist her with balancing the accounting. Status on filing of the petition to terminate conservatorship and of the final accounting due to the death of the Conservatee, and status on filing of the fourth and final accounting are continued to June 14, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Sep-22-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PVCN-13-296725 Conservatorship of Thea Archilletti and
PTR-14-398368 The Thea T. Archilletti Revocable Trust.

Appearances: Ernest Der appeared by zoom for and with Paula Bibbs by zoom.

The Court asked how the personal injury claim got settled without Court approval. Mr. Der stated that Ms. Bibbs worked with the insurance company to resolve the matter and that he wasn't aware of the settlement at that time. Ms. Bibbs stated that she did not realize she needed to seek Court approval, that she knows that now, that it won't happen again and that she is very aware. The Court asked Mr. Der when he thinks the trust can be closed. Mr. Der stated that the house has to get sold because there is a reverse mortgage, that he sent a re-appraisal for sale to the Probate Referee, that it will take 90-days to get to the point of sale and for the sale to close, and that it will take some months down the line to close the trust. Ms. Bibbs stated that she is already in communication with a Realtor. The Amended Third Account of Trustee, etc. and the Fourth Report of Conservator on Waiver of Account, etc. are under submission. Status on filing of the final accountings in both matters is set for December 21, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Helen Trowbridge. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Authority To Obtain A Reverse Mortgage And Encumber Trust Property And To Liquidate Annuity Account
Off Calendar Jul-28-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-13-296725 Conservatorship of Thea Archilletti and
PTR-14-298368 The Thea T. Archilletti Revocable Trust.

Appearances: Ernest Der appeared by zoom for and with Paula Bibbs (by zoom).

Mr. Der stated that they filed a petition in the related conservatorship matter with a hearing date in August which can be used as an offset of the overpayment. The Court stated that this is not an appropriate way to deal with this and that the proper way is to return that money to the trust. Ms. Bibbs stated that she has not been paid by the Court since
September of last year and that she does not have the funds to pay the trust right now. The Court stated that it will allow it, but in the future, it is to be handled as described. Mr. Der stated that the appropriate amount of bond will be based on what they actually receive from the mortgage and what is left in the estate, that they will submit an ex parte petition to increase bond and that the guestimate is about $150,000.00 to $200,000.00. Mr. Der also stated that a lot of the reverse mortgages are accruing interest, but the interest goes unpaid and gets added to the principal, and that they need 30 to 45-days to close. The Petitions for Authority to Obtain a Reverse Mortgage and Encumber Trust Property and to Liquidate Annuity Account, and the Petition for Approval of Reverse Mortgage are under submission. Status on filing of increased bond is set for August 24, 2020 in Dept. 204 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Authority To Obtain A Reverse Mortgage And Encumber Trust Property
Off Calendar Jan-03-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PCN-13-296725 Conservatorship of Thea Archilletti & PTR-14-298368 Thea Archilletti Revocable Trust
Appearances: Ernest Der for and with Paula Bibbs. Court Appointed Counsel Rowena Navia for Thea Archilletti.

The Court stated that this is the conservator and trustee's petitions for a reverse mortgage, that the property is held in the trust and asked if there are any objections. Ms. Navia stated that she has spoken with Ms. Archilletti and that she is excited that this will provide funds to purchase a lift that will enable her to get out of the house and that it will greatly enhance her life. The Court stated that if the conservatee outlives the money from the reverse mortgage the options will become very restrictive. Ms. Navia stated that Ms. Bibbs has caregivers at a good rate taking care of the conservatee. The Court asked why petitions were filed in both cases. Mr. Der stated that the lenders want orders for both, that he does not know of a good reason and that he expects that it is a comfort level for them. The Court stated that it is not inclined to let them dictate court procedure. Mr. Der stated that there are certain elements that pertain to each case in underwriting such as counseling to the conservatee. The Court stated that it is not clear why the trustee could not counsel the conservatee. The Court granted the petitions for approval of reverse mortgage (subject to review of the order) for one or both of the petitions, that it does not know that it makes sense to authorize the conservator to sign loan documents for property that is in a trust and that it does not want to expand the conservators role beyond the statutory requirements. Mr. Der stated that the trustee is unable to speak for the conservatee.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Ideta. (Not Reported)
